div1.entryFree:
Betide, partic. betid: Tp. I, 2, 31. R2 V, 1, 42. Cymb. IV, 3, 40. 1) intr. to happen, to come to pass: what news else --th here, Gentl. I, 1, 59. tales of woeful ages long ago betid, R2 V, 1, 42. a salve for any sore that may b. H6C IV, 6, 88. Followed by to: there is not so much perdition as an hair betid to any, Tp. I, 2, 31. and so b. to me as well I tender you, R3 II, 4, 71. what is betid to Cloten, Cymb. IV, 3, 40. Followed by of (== to become): if he were dead, what would b. of me? R3 I, 3, 6.
2) trans., to happen to, to befall: what --th me, Gentl. IV, 3, 40. woe b. thee, Tit. IV, 2, 56. more health and happiness b. my liege, R2 III, 2, 91. H6B I, 4, 69. R3 I, 2, 17. R3 I, 2, 17
